old_claim.note: Loom provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No textile-loom dependency remains because families and bridge builders twist and braid the grass ropes by hand before assembling the bridge.

source_shape.source_locator: Element description gives the sequence as hand-cutting and twisting straw, combining ropes, braiding six large cables, tying them to stone bases, and weaving the bridge in place.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Community members manufacture and assemble the structural ropes manually during the three-day renewal without a warp-weighted textile loom.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source's use of weaving as an assembly verb does not imply the separate archaeological loom technology represented by the prerequisite node.

ontology_before: Any object described as woven inherited a textile-loom dependency.
ontology_after: The bridge uses hand-twisted and braided rope construction without laundering textile equipment into its graph path.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: rope_bridges no longer directly depends on loom.
would_reject_if: Documentation showed that Q'eswachaka's ropes or deck are produced on a mechanical textile loom.
short_reason: The bridge is hand twisted and braided; no loom is used.
